persons.arguments_against: Strongest counter-hypothesis: another E. C. F. Garraway among the 20 Garraway officials (e.g. a son), or a successor Resident Commissioner being conflated. This fails on several independent dimensions: the biography prints 'res. comsrr., Basutoland, 16th Dec., 1917' — the exact unique office and territory of the stint, held by one person at a time; the staff list's rank column reads 'Lieut.-Col.' matching the bio's 'LT.-COL. SIR E. C. F.'; the honours track the bio's award dates independently — C.M.G. in the 1920-1922 rows (bio: C.M.G. 1911) upgrading to K.C.M.G. with the 'Sir' prefix by 1924 (bio: K.C.M.G. 1922); all three initials agree; and the listing ends in 1925, immediately before the bio's printed retirement 'ret., 19th Apr., 1926.' A namesake would have to share the same three initials, rank, honour sequence, and unique office in the same years — not realistic. The position_sim FAIL (58 vs bar 60) is a string artifact: 'res. comsrr.' is the standard abbreviation of 'Resident Commissioner'.
persons.would_overturn: Evidence that a different E. C. F. Garraway held the Basutoland residency 1920-1925, or that the biography subject left the post before 1920 — both contradicted by the printed 1917 appointment and 1926 retirement.

notes: Five co-occurring edition-years; unique office (Resident Commissioner, Basutoland) printed verbatim in the bio with appointment date 1917; rank (Lieut.-Col.) and the C.M.G.-to-K.C.M.G./'Sir' transition in 1924 independently corroborate; listing ends consistent with the April 1926 retirement.
